action.content:
Correctly close a Dyck-n word.

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: [ { [
A: ] } ]

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: < > ( ( [ [ ( { } ) [ < > ] ]
A: ] ) )

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: < [ < [ { < [ ] < { } > > } ] > { { ( ) } { < [ < > ] > }
A: } ] >

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: [ ( [
A:
action.label: context

action.content:
To complete the sequence `[ ( [`, we need to close the parentheses in the reverse order of their opening (Last-In, First-Out).

1.  The sequence starts with an opening square bracket: `[`. Stack: `[`
2.  Next is an opening parenthesis: `(`. Stack: `[`, `(`
3.  Next is an opening square bracket: `[`. Stack: `[`, `(`, `[`

To close them properly, we must pop the most recent open bracket first:
1.  Close the last `[` with `]`.
2.  Close the `(` with `)`.
3.  Close the first `[` with `]`.

The correct closing sequence is: **] ) ]**
action.label: response
